chunk_summary: Guidance on India-adapted vitamin D dosing for preterm/LBW/ELBW infants, including weight-based recommendations, monitoring strategies, and adjustments for special conditions like cholestasis or metabolic bone disease.
learning_objectives: Understand India-specific vitamin D dosing guidelines for preterm and low birth weight infants.
Learn how to monitor and adjust vitamin D supplementation based on weight, diet, and clinical conditions.
Recognize the importance of accounting for fortified formula and avoiding vitamin D overdosing.
Identify monitoring strategies for high-risk infants, including those with cholestasis or metabolic bone disease.
key_facts: India-specific guidelines recommend 400 IU/day for LBW infants (1500–2499 g) and 800 IU/day for VLBW infants (<1500 g) up to 6 months.
ESPGHAN suggests 400–700 IU/kg/day for stable preterm infants, with a maximum of 1000 IU/day.
Vitamin D sufficiency is defined as serum 25-hydroxyvitamin D >50 nmol/L, severe deficiency <25 nmol/L, and levels >120 nmol/L should be avoided.
High-risk infants (e.g., cholestasis, prolonged PN, or metabolic bone disease) may require higher doses and closer monitoring.
Vitamin D from fortified formula should be accounted for when determining supplementation needs.
reference_quote: Indian facility guidance commonly uses 400 IU/day for LBW infants 1500–2499 g and 800 IU/day for VLBW infants <1500 g, continued to 6 months.
document_key_topics: India-adapted vitamin D dosing for preterm/LBW/ELBW infants
Monitoring and adjusting vitamin D supplementation
